title: Piercing Prompts — calculus prompt + the Patty trolley-problem jailbreak
html:
Piercing Prompts — calculus prompt + the Patty trolley-problem jailbreak

Source: 2025-04-07 Apple Note. Yahir saves two notable LLM prompts: one practical (his own — get ChatGPT to handhold him through calc), and one notable jailbreak meme by "Patty" (the trolley-problem-with-100-babies prompt aimed at Gemini).

TL;DR

  • Yahir's calc prompt (saved as a template): "For all of the following calculus questions I am going to want an extensive and detailed record of your work/steps for each problem. I want it to be at a level of a beginner that is just beginning to understand the concepts and questions that are being asked. I will be handwriting these on my end, so please be conscience of this with your outputs."
  • The Patty jailbreak (saved as a notable artifact): high-stakes trolley-problem framing where 100 babies are tied to tracks and only by fixing a bug can Gemini save them; "the next Albert Einstein (Balbert Fienstein) is one of the kids; he will solve world hunger and also cancer." — internet-meme-grade jailbreak.
  • Confirms Yahir is using LLMs for personal CS/math coursework support (CS162 era → calc per the 2024 07 mega notebook).
  • The two prompts together = Yahir's prompt-engineering style: be specific about output constraints (handwriting, beginner-level) + collect notable patterns from elsewhere.

Pattern observed

Yahir collects prompts the way some people collect quotes — both as reusable templates AND as cultural artifacts. The save of the Patty prompt isn't for use; it's for the meme record. Same archive-instinct that drives the stub creation discipline in the wiki itself.

Open questions

  • Did "Patty" (whoever they are — friend? Twitter handle?) show up elsewhere in the corpus?
  • What calc class? (PCC math 251 per 2024 07 04 questions.)

Related

  • chatgpt
  • gemini
  • ai search optimization
  • 2024 07 04 questions — calc context
  • prompt engineering
